medical_history.history_of_present_illness: This is a ___ year-old male with obesity, hypertension, type II diabetes mellitus, hyperlipidemia, a history of cath in ___ (showing diffuse distal circumflex disease and LAD stenosis). He presented to PCP ___ ___ reporting palpitations and chest discomfort that he described as non-radiating pressure which occurred the night before and lasted 5 hours. Along w/ chest pressure he had experienced nausea and diaphoresis. PCP recommended that he go to ED which he would not do, though he agreed to stress test. He underwent stress test the following day which showed anterolateral wall ischemia and EF 52%, and subsequently he was referred to the ED where peak troponin was 0.48, ruling him in for NSTEMI. Started on heparin gtt, plavis, aspirin, high dose statin, atenolol. Cath at outside hospital showed tight lesion in the right coronary artery. He was transferred to ___ for PCI. Repeat catheterization here resulted in drug eluting stent to the native right coronary artery. On the floor post-procedure, the patient had no chest pain, shortness of breath, nausea, or diaphoresis. There is no history of exertional dyspnea, paroxysmal nocturnal dyspnea, orthopnea, presyncope, syncope, or palpitations.
medical_history.past_medical_history: CABG ___: LIMA-LAD, SVG to ramus intermedius, Type II Diabetes Mellitus, managed on insulin therapy, Hypercholesterolemia, Hypertension, Obesity, Tonsillectomy and adenoidectomy, Melanoma s/p resection ___, Coronary artery disease, s/p cath in ___ LAD stenosis and diffuse distal circumflex disease s/p tibial fx
medical_history.family_history: mother with several strokes and hypertension, death at age ___.

physical_examination.general_examination: VS - T: 97.3 BP 156/58 HR: 71 RR: 16 O2: 96% RA. Gen: Obese caucasian male NAD. Oriented x3. Mood, affect appropriate.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: Neurological: Not specified. Cardiovascular: PMI located in ___ intercostal space, midclavicular line. RR, normal S1, S2. No m/r/g. No thrills, lifts. No S3 or S4. Chest: No chest wall deformities, scoliosis or kyphosis. Resp were unlabored, no accessory muscle use. CTAB, no crackles, wheezes or rhonchi. Abd: +bs, Soft, NT, ND, obese. Ext: Trace bilateral edema. No femoral bruits. No palpable thrill or pulsatile mass. Skin: No stasis dermatitis, ulcers, scars, or xanthomas. Pulses: Right: 2+ Femoral DP 2+ ___ 2+. Left: Femoral 2+ DP 2+ ___ 2+.
